ksa_text: Ability to design and develop secure applications using secure development methodologies, tools, and frameworks.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Security & Platform Strategy is a primary or secondary domain for the Chief of Staff to CDAIO. This ability KSA is within scope for the role's expected competency profile and seniority level (senior specialist).

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (Security & Platform Strategy), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Framework signal is weak (composite 0.43). External validation of this ability KSA for the Chief of Staff to CDAIO role relies primarily on domain-level alignment rather than specific framework matches.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: NIST CSF, CMU SEI enterprise security frameworks, and CDAIO job market data consistently include security and platform strategy oversight for senior data leaders.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: domain-level relevance of Security & Platform Strategy for the Chief of Staff to CDAIO is corroborated by DAMA-DMBOK, CDO survey data, and role-specific job market analysis, despite weak cross-framework signal.

decision_rationale: Security & Platform Strategy ability KSA is within scope for the Chief of Staff to CDAIO at the senior specialist tier. Seniority fit is appropriate. All three evaluation passes support inclusion.
